tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic-range proteinuria and dehydration or catabolic state. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and low HbA1c.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
